pi_items.verified_value: Real ICMP traffic captured from actual network
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 18-46: Uses tcpdump to capture live traffic (-i any -w file icmp), then executes actual ping and traceroute commands to generate real ICMP packets on the network

Derived from traffic generation: (1) ping creates Type 8/0 pairs, (2) traceroute creates Type 11, (3) pings to unreachable address create Type 3. Verified in verifier.py lines 141-154 which checks for these 4 types

pi_items.metadata_value: Calculated from captured PCAP using tshark

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 59-96: Uses tshark to filter and analyze the captured PCAP file, computing 5 ground truth metrics stored as text files

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 89-91: tshark -Y 'icmp.type==11' -T fields -e ip.src, then sort/uniq/wc to count unique router hops

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 94-96: tshark -Y 'icmp.type==3' -T fields -e ip.dst, then sort/uniq to extract unreachable destinations

privileged_info_summary: The icmp_diagnostic_analysis task requires analyzing ICMP traffic captured in a PCAP file using Wireshark. The task.json file is incomplete/malformed with no metadata section. The task generates REAL (not synthetic) ICMP network traffic using tcpdump, ping, and traceroute commands targeting 8.8.8.8 (Google Public DNS) and 198.51.100.1 (TEST-NET-2 reserved documentation address per RFC 5737). Ground truth is computed from the captured packets using tshark to extract: total packet count, ICMP type counts (Types 0, 8, 11, 3), average RTT for Echo Reply pairs, unique traceroute hops from Time Exceeded messages, and unreachable destination IPs. The verifier scores based on: file creation (10pts), total count accuracy ±2 (10pts), each ICMP type accuracy ±2 (20pts), RTT accuracy ±20% (25pts), hop count ±1 (15pts), unreachable IP identification (10pts), and VLM visual confirmation (10pts). Network connectivity to 8.8.8.8 is required.
